messages.content: We refunded a customer their full payment. Do we still owe Razorpay its processing fee?

Yes. Clause 3.4 states that Razorpay PA fees remain applicable and payable on each transaction even if you refunded that transaction to your customer, including through normal refund channels or Razorpay’s instant refund service.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.4, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You agree that Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded the same to Your customer either through normal channels of refunds or through the instant refund service of Razorpay PA Group Entities (if availed).

known_facts.fact: You refunded the customer their full payment.
The question concerns whether Razorpay’s processing fee remains payable after a refund.

messages.content: A customer paid us through UPI, but we never captured the late-authorized payment. What happens to the money?
Under Clause 3.5, if a payment is late-authorized but not captured by you, Razorpay PA may initiate an auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.5, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You acknowledge and agree that for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You, Razorpay PA may initiate auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days.

known_facts.fact: The payment was late-authorized and was not captured by the merchant.

messages.content: If someone on our team leaks our Razorpay API credentials and fraudulent transactions happen, can we say Razorpay is responsible because the payments ran on its platform?

No. Under Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, you are responsible for keeping Razorpay API credentials and other secure credentials confidential, and you are responsible for use of the Platform or Services by anyone using those credentials, including unauthorized access and fraudulent transactions. The same clause says Razorpay PA is not liable for actions performed using your Secure Credentials. In addition, Part B, Part I, Clause 4.4 states that Razorpay PA is not responsible for liability arising from Fraudulent Transactions, whether domestic or international.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You are responsible for maintaining the secrecy of all Your passwords, keys, security codes, login and account information (“Secure Credentials”) associated with Your account with Razorpay PA and API access.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You will be responsible for the use of the Platform and/ or Services by You or anyone using Your Secure Credentials including but not limited to any unauthorized access, fraudulent transactions, or loss of data resulting from Your failure to maintain the confidentiality of Your Secure Credentials.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You are solely responsible for all activities that occur under Your account/in using Your Secure Credentials and Razorpay PA shall not be liable for any such change or action performed by using Your Secure Credentials on the Website.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.4, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You acknowledge that Razorpay PA shall not be responsible for any liability arising in respect of Fraudulent Transactions whether it is an international or a domestic transaction.

known_facts.fact: Someone on the user's team leaked the user's Razorpay API credentials.
Fraudulent transactions occurred using Razorpay's platform.

messages.content: A customer says their card was used without permission. Can Razorpay freeze our settlement money?

It depends on who reported it to Razorpay. Under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.1, if Razorpay PA is intimated by a Facility Provider that a customer reported an unauthorised debit of the customer’s Payment Instrument, Razorpay PA may suspend settlements to you while the inquiries, investigations, and resolution by the Facility Providers are pending. Was Razorpay PA informed by a Facility Provider about the customer’s unauthorised-debit report?

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.1, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: if Razorpay PA is intimated, by a Facility Provider, that a customer has reported an unauthorised debit of the customer's Payment Instrument (“Fraudulent Transaction”), then in addition to its rights under clause 16 of Part A: General Terms and Conditions, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution thereof by the Facility Providers.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 22, DEFINITIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: “Payment Instrument” includes credit card, debit card, bank account, prepaid payment instrument or any other instrument issued under Applicable Law, used by a customer to pay the Transaction Amount.

known_facts.fact: A customer says their card was used without permission.
The issue concerns a card transaction.

missing_facts.fact: Whether Razorpay PA was intimated by a Facility Provider that the customer reported an unauthorised debit of the customer’s Payment Instrument.
missing_facts.why_it_matters: Clause 4.1 gives Razorpay PA the right to suspend settlements during pending inquiries, investigations, and resolution only where Razorpay PA is intimated by a Facility Provider about such an unauthorised debit report.

clarifying_questions.question: Was Razorpay PA informed by a Facility Provider, such as a bank or card network, that the customer reported an unauthorised debit?

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: Razorpay PA was intimated by a Facility Provider that the customer reported an unauthorised debit of the customer’s Payment Instrument.
conditional_outcomes.outcome: Razorpay PA is entitled to suspend settlements to you during the pendency of inquiries, investigations, and resolution by the Facility Providers.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: Razorpay PA was not intimated by a Facility Provider, or that fact is not established.
conditional_outcomes.outcome: The specific settlement-suspension right in Clause 4.1 is not triggered based on the stated facts alone.

messages.content: A fraudulent transaction happened and Razorpay already settled the money to us. What happens now?

Since Razorpay PA has already settled the amount to you, Clause 4.2 says any post-settlement dispute about that Fraudulent Transaction will be resolved under the specified RBI notifications. If the Fraudulent Transaction has resulted in a Chargeback, Clause 4.3 says the Chargeback provisions in the Terms apply; under Clause 2.3, Razorpay PA may recover the Chargeback Amount from future settlements or other funds it holds, or issue a debit note if those funds are insufficient. Has the Facility Provider/Razorpay notified you of a Chargeback or charged a Chargeback Amount to Razorpay PA for this transaction?

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.2, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: If the amount in respect of the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to You pursuant to these Terms, any dispute arising in relation to the said Fraudulent Transaction, following settlement, shall be resolved in accordance with the RBI's notification DBR.No.Leg.BC.78/09.07.005/2017-18, dated July 6, 2017 read with RBI's notification DBOD. LEG. BC 86/09.07.007/2001-02 dated April 8, 2002, and other notifications, circulars and guidelines issued by the RBI in this regard from time to time.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.3, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Subject to clause 4.2 above, if the Fraudulent Transaction results in a Chargeback, then such Chargeback shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions set out in the Terms.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 2.3, CHARGEBACKS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Notwithstanding anything in these Terms, if the Facility Providers charge the Chargeback Amount from Razorpay PA then You agree and acknowledge that Razorpay PA is entitled to recover such Chargeback Amount from You by way of deduction from (i) the Transaction Amounts to be settled to You and (ii) any of Your other funds held by Razorpay PA in the course of providing the Services. Provided however, if the available Transaction Amounts or other funds are insufficient for deduction of the Chargeback Amount, then Razorpay PA is entitled to issue a debit note seeking reimbursement of the Chargeback Amount. You shall reimburse the Chargeback Amount within seven (7) days of receipt of the debit note.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 2.1, CHARGEBACKS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: If a Facility Provider communicates to Razorpay PA the receipt of a Chargeback Request, You will be notified of the Chargeback.

known_facts.fact: Razorpay already settled the money to the user.

missing_facts.fact: Whether the Fraudulent Transaction has resulted in a Chargeback or a Facility Provider has charged the Chargeback Amount from Razorpay PA.
missing_facts.why_it_matters: This determines whether the Chargeback provisions and recovery mechanics apply in addition to the post-settlement dispute rule.

clarifying_questions.question: Has the Facility Provider/Razorpay notified you of a Chargeback or charged a Chargeback Amount to Razorpay PA for this transaction?

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The fraudulent-transaction amount has already been settled to you.
conditional_outcomes.outcome: Any post-settlement dispute about that Fraudulent Transaction is to be resolved in accordance with the specified RBI notifications; the Terms do not spell out the detailed regulatory outcome.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The Fraudulent Transaction results in a Chargeback.

applies_when.mf_001: A Chargeback has resulted from the Fraudulent Transaction.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: The Chargeback is resolved under the Terms. If Facility Providers charge the Chargeback Amount from Razorpay PA, Razorpay PA may recover it from amounts to be settled to you or other funds it holds; if those funds are insufficient, Razorpay PA may issue a debit note seeking reimbursement, payable within seven days of receipt.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: A Facility Provider communicates a Chargeback Request to Razorpay PA.

applies_when.mf_001: A Facility Provider has communicated receipt of a Chargeback Request to Razorpay PA.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: You will be notified of the Chargeback.

messages.content: We want to let a new merchant category collect payments through us on Razorpay. Is that allowed?

I can’t determine that from the question alone. Under Part A, Clause 2.14.item005, use of the Services must not facilitate prohibited products or services, and Part A, Clause 16.1.item008 addresses materially changing the products/services declared at onboarding without Razorpay PA’s prior written permission. Please specify the exact merchant category/products or services, and whether this is a change from what was declared during onboarding.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.14.item005, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Your use of Services does not facilitate the offer, sale or purchase of prohibited products and/or services specified under these Terms.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1.item008, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You materially change the type of the products/services provided to end customers and as declared on the onboarding form, without obtaining Razorpay PA's prior written permission to use the Services for the new or changed types of services/products

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.1, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Adult goods and services which includes pornography and other sexually suggestive materials (including literature, imagery and other media); escort or prostitution services; website access and/or website memberships of pornography or illegal sites;

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.2, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Alcohol which includes alcohol or alcoholic beverages such as beer, liquor, wine, or champagne;

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.14, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Gaming/gambling which includes lottery tickets, sports bets, memberships/ enrolment in online gambling sites, and related content;

known_facts.fact: The user wants to let a new merchant category collect payments through Razorpay.

missing_facts.fact: The exact merchant category and the products or services that the new merchants will offer, including whether this differs from what was declared on the onboarding form.
missing_facts.why_it_matters: Razorpay’s Terms prohibit use of the Services for prohibited products/services, and a material change in the type of products/services from what was declared at onboarding may require Razorpay PA’s prior written permission.

clarifying_questions.question: What exact products or services will this new merchant category offer, and is it a change from the products/services declared on the onboarding form?

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The new category involves products or services prohibited by the Terms.

applies_when.mf_001: The merchant category includes prohibited products/services, such as adult goods/services, alcohol, or gaming/gambling as described in the cited prohibited-products clauses.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: Use of the Services for that category would not be allowed under the cited Terms.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The new category is a material change from the products/services declared at onboarding.

applies_when.mf_001: The merchant materially changes the type of products/services provided to end customers from what was declared on the onboarding form.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: The Terms indicate that Razorpay PA’s prior written permission is required to use the Services for the new or changed types of products/services.

messages.content: We are launching a real-money fantasy sports feature, but we think it is skill-based. Can we use Razorpay to collect entry fees?

I cannot give an unconditional yes from the supplied ToS alone. The Terms expressly prohibit using Razorpay for acceptance of payments related to “any form of real money online gaming” or an “online money game/online money game service” as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025 (Part A, Clause 14.10). The ToS does not provide the legal classification of a skill-based real-money fantasy sports product, so whether your feature falls within those regulated categories depends on external regulation. If it does, you cannot use Razorpay to collect entry fees. The prohibited-products list also flags gaming/gambling, including sports bets, and businesses operating under legally ambiguous laws.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 14.10, ADDITIONAL TERMS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You expressly covenant and warrant that You shall not use the Services or the Platform for the sale, promotion, facilitation, or acceptance of payments related to any form of real money online gaming or an online money game/online money game service as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.14, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Gaming/gambling which includes lottery tickets, sports bets, memberships/ enrolment in online gambling sites, and related content;

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.35, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Businesses or website that operate within the scope of laws which are not absolutely clear or are ambiguous in nature (e.g. web-based telephony, Website supplying medicines or controlled substances, website that promise online matchmaking);

known_facts.fact: The merchant is launching a real-money fantasy sports feature.
The merchant wants to collect entry fees through Razorpay.
The merchant believes the feature is skill-based.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: The ToS prohibits certain real-money online gaming and online money game categories by reference to external regulation, but the supplied ToS text does not determine whether a skill-based real-money fantasy sports feature falls within those categories.

messages.content: Razorpay suspended our account under Clause 16. How long can it hold our settlement funds?

The supplied ToS provisions do not state a fixed maximum period for holding settlement funds after a Clause 16 suspension. Clause 16.1 gives Razorpay PA the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement of monies/payments in specified events, but it does not specify how long the settlement suspension may last. Related clauses make settlement subject to risk/compliance checks, external instructions, applicable rules, and Razorpay PA discretion; in some cases Razorpay PA may withhold settlement and refund funds to the source account after prior notice. So, based only on these records, the duration is not determinable from the ToS.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement of any monies or payments to You, without any liability to You

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1.item001, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You breach any clause of these Terms.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1.item003, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA receives instructions from Facility Providers or governmental authorities or law enforcement agencies to either suspend the Services, or part thereof, or directs to suspend the Services or part thereof

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 1.2, PAYMENT PROCESSING
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You acknowledge and agree that the foregoing is subject to credit to / receipt of funds by Razorpay PA in the Escrow Account from our Facility Providers and risk/compliance check of the Transaction by Razorpay and/or Facility Providers.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 1.16, PAYMENT PROCESSING
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold such settlement and after giving prior notice to you, shall refund the said amount back to the source account from which it was received.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.8, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Your failure to submit the KYC Documents when requisitioned shall entitle Razorpay PA to suspend the Services and/or stop settlement of monies (as applicable) until You submit such KYC Documents to the sole satisfaction of Razorpay PA.

known_facts.fact: The user's account was suspended under Clause 16.
The user wants to know how long Razorpay can hold settlement funds after the suspension.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: Clause 16.1 authorizes immediate suspension of settlement, but the supplied Clause 16 records do not define any maximum duration, release deadline, or holding period for settlement funds. Other settlement clauses refer to risk/compliance checks, applicable guidelines, external instructions, and Razorpay PA discretion, which leaves the duration unresolved from the ToS text supplied.

messages.content: What exact fraud threshold triggers NPCI-related consequences for us under the Razorpay terms?

The Razorpay Terms do not state the exact fraud amount threshold. Part B, Part I, Clause 4.5 says liability is triggered by breach of fraud amount thresholds provided under the NPCI guideline on “Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions” NPCI/2022-23/RMD/001, and that the decision of NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank is final and binding. Because the actual threshold is incorporated by reference to an external NPCI guideline and is not reproduced in the supplied ToS text, the exact threshold cannot be answered from the Terms alone.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.5, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You shall be liable in the event of breach of the fraud amount thresholds as provided under the NPCI guideline on ‘Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions' NPCI/2022- 23/RMD/001. You hereby understand and agree that the decision of the NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank, as the case may be, shall be final and binding.

known_facts.fact: The user is asking for the exact fraud threshold that triggers NPCI-related consequences under the Razorpay Terms.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: Clause 4.5 references fraud amount thresholds contained in an external NPCI guideline, but the supplied ToS text does not specify the exact threshold.
